• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • Download Rmd

06_trophic_niches_from_stable_isotopes

Peter Flood

2025-09-09

Install and load libraries

Install if needed

# install.packages("remotes")      # Install remotes if needed
# remotes::install_github("clquezada/tRophicPosition")
if (!require("SIBER")) install.packages("SIBER")
if (!require("ellipse")) install.packages("ellipse")
if (!require("ggpubr")) install.packages("ggpubr")
if (!require("openxlsx")) install.packages("openxlsx")
if (!require("coda")) install.packages("coda")
if (!require("ggpubr")) install.packages("ggpubr")
if (!require("tidyverse")) install.packages("tidyverse")

Load

library(SIBER) #modeling isotopic niches
library(ellipse) #extracting posterior ellipses
library(openxlsx) #importing and exporting excel files
library(coda) #Bayesian model diagnostics
library(tRophicPosition) #using their pairwise comparisons functions 
library(ggpubr) #plotting, arranging multiple ggplots 
library(tidyverse) #data manipulation and plotting via ggplot

Load and format data

Load data and filter to herps

all.isotopes <- read.csv(file = "Data/Stable Isotopes/FCE1259_isotope_merge_all.csv", header = T)

herp.isotopes <- all.isotopes %>%
  filter(size_class %in% c("A.gry1", "A.mea1", "N.flo1", "N.vir1", "P.axa1", "R.gry1", "R.gry2", "S.lac1"))

Format for SIBER

#prepare data for SIBER
#need to unite the habitat and season columns into a single column called "community"
iso.slough.hab.season<-unite(herp.isotopes, col = "community", Slough, Habitat, Season, sep ="_") %>% 
  mutate(size_class = case_when(
    is.na(size_class) ~ Species,
    !is.na(size_class) ~ size_class
  ))

#isolate only the data needed for SIBER
iso.siber<-select(iso.slough.hab.season, d13C, d15N, size_class, community)
colnames(iso.siber)<-c("iso1", "iso2", "group", "community")

#dropping consumers where n < 3 or sd of either isotope is 0
siber.data<-iso.siber %>%
  group_by(group, community) %>%
  filter(n()>2, sd(iso1)!=0, sd(iso2)!=0, !is.na(iso1), !is.na(iso2)) %>% #remove groups with n < 3 and those that didn't get C or N peaks
  ungroup() %>%
  as.data.frame() %>% #SIBER needs this step to work 
  arrange(community, group) #this step makes it so colnames() of ML output are the same order as ellipse model output
#check what was removed
removed.consumers<-iso.siber[!(iso.siber$group %in% siber.data$group),]

#createSiberObject
global.iso.siber<-createSiberObject(siber.data)
Warning: At least one of your groups has less than 5 observations.
          The absolute minimum sample size for each group is 3 in order
          for the various ellipses and corresponding metrics to be 
          calculated. More reasonably though, a minimum of 5 data points
          are required to calculate the two means and the 2x2 covariance 
          matrix and not run out of degrees of freedom. Check the item 
          named 'sample.sizes' in the object returned by this function 
          in order to locate the offending group. Bear in mind that NAs in 
          the sample.size matrix simply indicate groups that are not 
          present in that community, and is an acceptable data structure 
          for these analyses.

SIBER models

Visually inspect data

# Create lists of plotting arguments to be passed onwards to each 
# of the three plotting functions.
community.hulls.args <- list(col = 1, lty = 1, lwd = 1)
group.ellipses.args  <- list(n = 100, p.interval = 0.95, lty = 1, lwd = 2)
group.hull.args      <- list(lty = 2, col = "grey20")

par(mfrow=c(1,1))
plotSiberObject(global.iso.siber,
                ax.pad = 2, 
                hulls = F, community.hulls.args, 
                ellipses = T, group.ellipses.args,
                group.hulls = T, group.hull.args,
                bty = "L",
                iso.order = c(1,2),
                xlab = expression(d13C),
                ylab = expression(d15N)
)

Calculate TA, SEA, SEAc

global.iso.ML <- groupMetricsML(global.iso.siber)

print(global.iso.ML)
     SRS_Marsh_Dry.N.flo1 SRS_Marsh_Dry.N.vir1 SRS_Marsh_Dry.R.gry1 SRS_Marsh_Dry.S.lac1 SRS_Pond_Dry.A.mea1
TA               1.056950             1.177450             4.896400             3.365200           0.1414000
SEA              1.341590             2.135658             2.884803             2.479092           0.2564712
SEAc             2.012385             4.271316             3.296918             2.892274           0.5129425
     SRS_Pond_Dry.R.gry2 SRS_Pond_Dry.S.lac1 SRS_Pond_Wet.A.gry1 SRS_Pond_Wet.N.vir1
TA              2.165250            6.910400            3.548650           0.1568000
SEA             1.741326            4.791122            5.156381           0.1643388
SEAc            2.176657            5.749346            7.734571           0.2465083

Set up model parameters

#set up the model parameters 
# options for running jags
parms <- list()
parms$n.iter <- 2 * 10^6   # number of iterations to run the model for
parms$n.burnin <- 1 * 10^4 # discard the first set of values
parms$n.thin <- 100     # thin the posterior by this many
parms$n.chains <- 2        # run this many chains
parms$save.output = T
parms$save.dir = getwd()

# define the priors
priors <- list()
priors$R <- 1 * diag(2)
priors$k <- 2
priors$tau.mu <- 1.0E-3

Run model

# fit the ellipses which uses an Inverse Wishart prior
# on the covariance matrix Sigma, and a vague normal prior on the 
# means. Fitting is via the JAGS method.
global.iso.ellipses.posterior <- siberMVN(global.iso.siber, parms, priors)
Compiling model graph
   Resolving undeclared variables
   Allocating nodes

#calculate posterior estimates of ellipses for all groups
SEA.B<-siberEllipses(global.iso.ellipses.posterior)
colnames(SEA.B)<-names(global.iso.ellipses.posterior)

Extract output

#calculate credibility intervals
cr.p<-c(0.5, 0.95, 0.99) #vector of desired quantiles

#call hdrcde_hdr using lapply()
SEA.B.credibles<-lapply(
  as.data.frame(SEA.B), 
  function(x,...){tmp<-hdrcde::hdr(x)$hdr},
  prob = cr.p)

#match up group names
names(SEA.B.credibles)<-colnames(global.iso.ML)

#this carries through the row names from matrices in SEA.B.credibles
#they don't turn out all that clean though, so adding a column later with the same info
y <- do.call(function(...) {
  tmp <- plyr::rbind.fill.matrix(...)
  rownames(tmp) <- sapply(SEA.B.credibles, function(i) {
    rownames(i)
  })
  return(tmp)
}, SEA.B.credibles)

y
            1         2
99% 0.7918703 16.933169
95% 1.0605916 10.619254
50% 2.0183510  4.325288
99% 0.6417813 31.489098
95% 1.0602103 17.135852
50% 2.3304171  5.778542
99% 1.1428299  7.690467
95% 1.4007614  5.809314
50% 2.1503286  3.457638
99% 0.9055392  6.861014
95% 1.1183660  5.094135
50% 1.7461835  2.907944
99% 0.1753981  8.714515
95% 0.2821022  4.443847
50% 0.5934057  1.478185
99% 0.6830957  7.239465
95% 0.8537044  5.027214
50% 1.3967529  2.538427
99% 1.6110446 14.361328
95% 2.0286805 10.401918
50% 3.2756156  5.633094
99% 1.0371294 25.480964
95% 1.4820428 15.512916
50% 2.9940296  6.359812
99% 0.2557500  5.153032
95% 0.3388988  3.204686
50% 0.6150980  1.309161
#object with cleaned-up row names
interval<-rep(c("99%", "95%", "50%"), 9)

#replicated each name three times in sequence to match up with new data frame
seab.names<-data.frame(rep(names(SEA.B.credibles), each = 3))
colnames(seab.names)<-"names"

#seab.names<-separate(seab.names, col = names, into = c('hab.season', 'spp'), extra = "merge")

#combine names with new data frame
seab.cred.matrix<-data.frame(seab.names, interval, y)
colnames(seab.cred.matrix)<-c(colnames(seab.names), "interval", "lower", "upper")

#write out to a csv
write.csv(seab.cred.matrix, file = "Outputs/Stable Isotopes/Trophic Niche/global.sc.SEA.B.credibles.csv")

# do similar to get the modes, taking care to pick up multimodal posterior
# distributions if present
SEA.B.modes<-lapply(
  as.data.frame(SEA.B), 
  function(x,...){tmp<-hdrcde::hdr(x)$mode},
  prob = cr.p, all.modes=T)

#need to do the same as for the credible intervals to get the colnames to be
#the names not the numbers
write.csv(SEA.B.modes, file = "Outputs/Stable Isotopes/Trophic Niche/global.sc.SEA.B.modes.csv")

#read back in
SEA.B.modes.matrix<-read.csv(file = "Outputs/Stable Isotopes/Trophic Niche/global.sc.SEA.B.modes.csv", header = T, row.names = 1)

#change colnames
colnames(SEA.B.modes.matrix)<-colnames(global.iso.ML)
#transpose this matrix
SEA.B.modes.matrix.t<-t(SEA.B.modes.matrix)
#change the name of what is now a single column to mode
colnames(SEA.B.modes.matrix.t)<-"mode"
#write out the final csv over the previous mode csv
write.csv(SEA.B.modes.matrix.t, file = "Outputs/Stable Isotopes/Trophic Niche/global.sc.SEA.B.modes.csv")

Visual Niches

herp.ellipse.df <- siber.data %>% 
  separate(community, into = c("Slough", "Habitat", "Season"), sep = "_") %>% 
  rename(Species = group) %>% 
  mutate(Species = case_when(
      Species == "A.gry1" ~ "Cricket Frog Tadpoles",
      Species == "A.mea1" ~ "Two-Toed Amphiuma",
      Species == "N.flo1" ~ "Florida Green Watersnake", 
      Species == "N.vir1" ~ "Peninsula Newt",  
      Species == "R.gry1" ~ "Pig Frog Tadpoles", 
      Species == "R.gry2" ~ "Pig Frog",
      Species == "S.lac1" ~ "Greater Siren"
      ))

(herp.niche.plot <- ggplot(data = herp.ellipse.df, aes(x = iso1, y = iso2, color = Species, fill = Species))+
    scale_color_viridis_d(end = 0, begin = 0.8, option = "A")+
    scale_fill_viridis_d(end = 0, begin = 0.8, option = "A")+
    facet_grid(Season~Habitat)+
    stat_conf_ellipse(level = 0.95, geom = "polygon", alpha = 0.5)+
    geom_point()+
    #labs(x = "δ13C(‰)", y = "δ15N(‰)")+
    labs(y = "MDS2", x = "MDS1")+
    theme_bw()+
    theme(text = element_text(family = "Times", size = 12),
          #panel.spacing.x = unit(7, "mm"),
          panel.grid.major = element_blank(),panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
          strip.text.y.right = element_text(angle = 0),
          axis.text = element_text(size = 12))
  
)

ggsave("Figures/Stable Isotopes/Trophic Niche/herp_si_niche_plot.tiff", 
       herp.niche.plot, 
       height = 150, width = 200, units = "mm", dpi = 600)

Posterior mean ellipses

mean_ellipses <- lapply(names(global.iso.ellipses.posterior), function(name) {
  posterior <- global.iso.ellipses.posterior[[name]] %>% as.data.frame()
  #colnames(posterior) <- c("Sigma211", "Sigma212", "Sigma221", "Sigma222", "mu1", "mu2")
  # Use backticks for column names with [ or ] characters
  mu_mean <- c(mean(posterior$`mu[1]`), mean(posterior$`mu[2]`))
  Sigma_mean <- matrix(
    c(
      mean(posterior$`Sigma2[1,1]`),
      mean(posterior$`Sigma2[1,2]`),
      mean(posterior$`Sigma2[2,1]`),
      mean(posterior$`Sigma2[2,2]`)
    ),
    nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE
  )
  ell <- ellipse(Sigma_mean, centre = mu_mean, level = 0.4, npoints = 100)
  df_ell <- as.data.frame(ell)
  df_ell$group_comm <- name
  df_ell$group <- sub("\\..*", "", name)
  df_ell$community <- sub(".*\\.", "", name)
  df_ell
})

mean_ellipses_df <- do.call(rbind, mean_ellipses) %>% 
  rename(community = group, group = community) %>% 
  mutate(group = factor(group))

Ellipse Cloud Plot

n_ellipses <- 200

ellipse_clouds <- list()

for (name in names(global.iso.ellipses.posterior)) {
  posterior <- global.iso.ellipses.posterior[[name]]
  nrow_posterior <- nrow(posterior)
  set.seed(2024)
  sampled_rows <- sample(seq_len(nrow_posterior), n_ellipses)
  
  temp <- lapply(sampled_rows, function(i) {
    row <- posterior[i, ]
    mu <- c(row[["mu[1]"]], row[["mu[2]"]])
    # Make sure you get the elements in the correct order:
    Sigma <- matrix(
      c(row[["Sigma2[1,1]"]], row[["Sigma2[1,2]"]],
        row[["Sigma2[2,1]"]], row[["Sigma2[2,2]"]]),
      nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE
    )
    ell <- ellipse(Sigma, centre = mu, level = 0.40, npoints = 100)
    df <- as.data.frame(ell)
    df$group_comm <- name
    df$draw <- i
    df
  })
  ellipse_clouds[[name]] <- do.call(rbind, temp)
}

ellipse_cloud_df <- do.call(rbind, ellipse_clouds)

ellipse_cloud_df$community <- sub("\\..*", "", ellipse_cloud_df$group_comm) %>% factor()
ellipse_cloud_df$group <- sub(".*\\.", "", ellipse_cloud_df$group_comm) %>% 
  factor()

Plot Ellipse Cloud

#Format data for this plot
siber.plot.data <- siber.data %>%
      mutate(
        Species = group,
        Species = case_when(
          Species == "A.gry1" ~ "Cricket Frog Tadpoles",
          Species == "A.mea1" ~ "Two-Toed Amphiuma",
          Species == "N.flo1" ~ "Florida Green Watersnake",
          Species == "N.vir1" ~ "Peninsula Newt",
          Species == "R.gry1" ~ "Pig Frog Tadpoles",
          Species == "R.gry2" ~ "Pig Frog",
          Species == "S.lac1" ~ "Greater Siren"
        )
      ) %>% 
  separate(community, into = c("Slough", "Habitat", "Season"), sep = "_")

ellipse.cloud.df <- ellipse_cloud_df %>%
        mutate(
          Species = group,
          Species = case_when(
            group_comm == "SRS_Pond_Wet.A.gry1" ~ "Cricket Frog Tadpoles",
            Species == "mea1" ~ "Two-Toed Amphiuma",
            Species == "flo1" ~ "Florida Green Watersnake",
            Species == "vir1" ~ "Peninsula Newt",
            group_comm == "SRS_Marsh_Dry.R.gry1" ~ "Pig Frog Tadpoles",
            Species == "gry2" ~ "Pig Frog",
            Species == "lac1" ~ "Greater Siren"
          )
        ) %>% 
  separate(community, into = c("Slough", "Habitat", "Season"), sep = "_")

mean.ellipses.df <- mean_ellipses_df %>% 
  separate(community, into = c("Slough", "Habitat", "Season"), sep = "_") %>% 
  mutate(
          Species = group,
          Species = case_when(
            group_comm == "SRS_Pond_Wet.A.gry1" ~ "Cricket Frog Tadpoles",
            Species == "mea1" ~ "Two-Toed Amphiuma",
            Species == "flo1" ~ "Florida Green Watersnake",
            Species == "vir1" ~ "Peninsula Newt",
            group_comm == "SRS_Marsh_Dry.R.gry1" ~ "Pig Frog Tadpoles",
            Species == "gry2" ~ "Pig Frog",
            Species == "lac1" ~ "Greater Siren"
          )
        )
  


(
  ellipse.cloud.iso.plot <- ggplot(
    siber.plot.data,
    aes(
      x = iso1,
      y = iso2,
      color = Species,
      fill = Species
    )
  ) +
    geom_path(
      data = ellipse.cloud.df ,
      aes(
        x = x,
        y = y,
        group = interaction(group_comm, draw),
        color = Species
      ),
      size = 0.7,
      alpha = 0.05,
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_path(
      data = mean.ellipses.df,
      aes(
        x = x,
        y = y,
        group = group_comm,
        color = Species
      ),
      #color = "red",
      linetype = "dashed",
      size = 1.2,
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    stat_conf_ellipse(
      level = 0.95,
      geom = "polygon",
      alpha = 0.5
    ) +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
    labs(
      title = "Trophic Niches from Stable Isotopes",
      x = expression(paste(delta^{13}, "C (\u2030)")),
      y = expression(paste(delta^{15}, "N (\u2030)")),
      color = "Species",
      fill = "Species"
    ) +
    scale_color_viridis_d(
      end = 0,
      begin = 0.8,
      option = "A"
    ) +
    scale_fill_viridis_d(
      end = 0,
      begin = 0.8,
      option = "A"
    ) +
    facet_grid(Season ~ Habitat) +
    theme_bw() +
    coord_fixed(
      xlim = c(-36, -25), ylim = c(2, 12)
      )
)

ggsave("Figures/Stable Isotopes/Trophic Niche/herp_si_niches_plot.tiff", 
       ellipse.cloud.iso.plot, 
       height = 150, width = 200, units = "mm", dpi = 600)

Stack with Niche SCA

Stack with similar plot based on stomach contents Requires running 04_trophic_niche_from_stomch_contents first
